subject: Cloud Computing
title: EC2: The Service That Started It All
description: A fast breakdown of EC2: The Service That Started It All for builders.

hook.text: In 2006, AWS launched EC2 and let anyone rent a virtual server for 10 cents an hour.

buildup.text: EC2 offered dozens of instance types: tiny for testing, massive for databases, GPU for machine learning.

discovery.text: You choose your operating system, configure networking, and launch — a full server in under a minute.

twist.text: EC2 instances are ephemeral by default. If the hardware fails, your instance vanishes unless you prepared.

climax.text: Despite being nearly 20 years old, EC2 remains the single largest revenue source for AWS.

punchline.text: EC2 proved that renting servers could build an empire.

quiz.question: What is a key risk of using EC2 instances without proper preparation?
options: Instances can vanish if the underlying hardware fails
EC2 can only run Windows operating systems
Each instance is limited to exactly one hour of runtime
